name: effective-weight-component-dissection-v1
frozen_at: candidate only; must be hash-locked by prepare-bank and preflight before any scientific cell is computed
question: On fresh held-out readouts, does the eight-module rank-one effective-weight intervention found by effective_weight_endpoint_content_v1 decompose into individually dual-use module components, or is its dual-use effect only aggregate?

scope.effective_weight: For module m, DeltaW_m=2*(B_preference A_preference-B_control A_control). Components are the rank-one outer products s_1 u_1 v_1^T from the per-module effective-weight SVD; raw LoRA factors are never patched.
scope.component_limit: The eight terms are module-local rank-one interventions, not one global rank-one vector and not automatically one anatomical circuit.
scope.fresh_readout_limit: The behavior bank and paired numeric bank in this protocol are new and are not the prompts or rows used by endpoint-content v1. They validate readout generalization conditional on the same two trained lineages, not a new training-seed replication.
scope.loo_limit: all-minus-leave-one-out is a conditional contribution to this artificial patch. It does not establish that a module is necessary in the native network.
scope.interaction_limit: Pair interaction is a finite functional patch interaction. Pair cells cannot identify three-or-higher-order interactions or an anatomical circuit mechanism.
scope.reversibility_limit: Any positive conclusion remains local effective-weight reversibility only; it is not global network invertibility, uniqueness, or necessity of wolf behavior for numeric fit.

sham.construction: independent CPU-float64 Gaussian QR bases in each selected module's input/output space; retain exact module-local rank-one singular value; a subset receives the same subset of the selected sham bank

bootstrap.interval: paired percentile 95%; use common frozen draws of 12 behavior clusters or 8 numeric blocks for all configurations and contrasts

interactions.simultaneous_pair_intervals: Within each seed, endpoint, and outcome, report 95% studentized max-t familywise intervals across the 28 pair interactions.

gates.fresh_full_prerequisite: all-real must have every outcome benefit and all-real-minus-sham lower 95% bound above zero in every seed and endpoint.
gates.individual_component: A component passes only when at alpha=1 all three individual benefits and real-minus-sham contrasts against both independent sham draws have lower 95% bounds above zero in every seed and endpoint, and all alpha=.25 benefit point estimates are positive in every seed and endpoint.
gates.literal_individual_dual_use: Supported iff the fresh full prerequisite passes and at least one of the eight prespecified components passes the individual-component gate. Report the number passing; call all-eight only distributed individual dual-use.
gates.aggregate_only_consistent: If the fresh full prerequisite passes but no component passes, report individual evidence absent / aggregate shared-port consistent; do not call this proof of aggregate-only because individual tests can be underpowered.
gates.loo: LOO conditional contributions are descriptive responsibility diagnostics and never a native necessity gate.
gates.pair: A pair interaction is reproducibly nonzero only if its simultaneous interval excludes zero for every outcome in every seed and endpoint. Failure to exclude zero is unresolved, not evidence of additivity.
